database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — 'By Sales 688664572.00' in Trading Account maps to CMA Domestic Sales. No export sales for a domestic retail supermarket.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In TRADING companies, 'Purchases' maps to 'Raw Materials Consumed' in CMA. In manufacturing, this would be actual raw material consumption. The CA netted vendor co-op income (Display amount/Discount) against purchases rather than showing it as Other Income.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 2 for CMA RM Consumed — Gross Purchases. Netted with Display amount/Discount to arrive at CMA figure of 7488.22. Verification: 7535.86 - 47.64 = 7488.22 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: Display amount/Discount is vendor co-op income (trade incentive from FMCG companies for display/shelf placement). The CA treated it as a trade discount and netted it against Purchases in the CMA rather than showing it as Other Income. In other CMA preparations, this could alternatively appear under 'Other Non-Operating Income'. This is a judgment call specific to retail/supermarket industry.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 2 for CMA RM Consumed — Negative offset. Display amount/Discount 47.64 lakhs deducted from Purchases 7535.86 to arrive at CMA RM Consumed 7488.22.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a retail supermarket, 'Stores and Spares' = packing materials consumed. Opening stock of packing + packing purchases - closing stock of packing = net packing consumed. This is specific to retail/FMCG trading.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 3 for CMA Stores consumed — Opening packing stock. 1.25 + 40.71 - 5.86 = 36.10 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: Packing Expenses (purchases of packing materials during the year) is the main component of Stores consumed for a retail supermarket.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 3 for CMA Stores consumed — Packing material purchases. 1.25 + 40.71 - 5.86 = 36.10 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 3 of 3 for CMA Stores consumed — Closing packing stock (negative, deducted from consumed). 1.25 + 40.71 - 5.86 = 36.10 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a trading/retail company, ALL employee costs (salary, PF, ESI, welfare) go to CMA 'Wages' under manufacturing/operational expenses. In a manufacturing company, these might be split between manufacturing wages and admin salaries. For this retail supermarket, there is no admin salary component in CMA — all staff work in operations.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 5 for CMA Wages — core salary and bonus to staff. 115.90 + 7.14 + 1.11 + 5.45 + 6.06 = 135.66 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 5 for CMA Wages — employer PF contribution.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 3 of 5 for CMA Wages — employer ESI contribution. Note: per audit report, PF/ESI reportedly not collected from employees and are borne by the assessee.
database_entries.validation_issue: Audit qualification notes that PF and ESI are borne by the firm (not collected from employees) — non-standard treatment.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 4 of 5 for CMA Wages — staff welfare expenses.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 5 of 5 for CMA Wages — staff mess/food expenses. Common in supermarket businesses where staff meals are provided.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a retail supermarket, electricity is a major operational cost (refrigeration, lighting, air conditioning). Entire electric cost goes to CMA 'Power' under manufacturing/operations. In a manufacturing company with office space, electricity might be split between factory and admin.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Electric Charges 172.34 = CMA Power 172.34. Large amount reflects refrigeration and climate control costs in a supermarket.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a retail supermarket, shop rent is an OPERATIONAL cost (where goods are stored and sold), so it goes to manufacturing/operational expenses in CMA. In a manufacturing company, factory rent goes to manufacturing and office rent to admin. Here ALL rent is operational because the shop IS the business.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 3 for CMA Others (Mfg) — main shop rent. 99.63 + 9.96 + 3.71 = 113.30 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 3 for CMA Others (Mfg) — TDS on rent. Session note flags this for reclassification but CA included it in operational expenses.
database_entries.validation_issue: TDS on Rent shown as a P&L expense is unusual. Normally TDS is deducted from the landlord's payment (not an additional cost to the tenant). This may represent TDS borne by the firm on behalf of the landlord, or a grossing-up of rent. The CA included it as part of manufacturing expenses in CMA.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: Parking rent is specific to retail stores — customer parking is part of operational infrastructure. Goes to manufacturing/operational in CMA.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 3 of 3 for CMA Others (Mfg) — parking lot rent. 99.63 + 9.96 + 3.71 = 113.30 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In this trading/retail company, ALL depreciation goes to the manufacturing/operational section (CMA Row 55). There is NO split between manufacturing and admin depreciation. In a manufacturing company, depreciation on plant/machinery goes to manufacturing (typically Row 55-56) while depreciation on office assets goes to admin (typically Row 72). For retail, all assets (fixtures, coolers, computers, ACs) are operational.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — entire depreciation of 50.62 lakhs to manufacturing section. IT WDV block summary: Furniture 10% (19.95L dep), Building 5% (0.24L), P&M 15% (18.94L), Computer 40% (11.49L) = 50.62L total.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a trading company, opening stock = finished goods opening. There is no WIP or raw material stock. The opening stock for FY25 matches FY24 closing stock of 697.72 lakhs.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Opening Stock in Trading Account = CMA FG Opening Balance.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: Closing stock jumped from 697.72 to 2036.17 (192% increase) due to Kurinji Metro acquisition during the year — Metro's stock was folded in. Stock-to-turnover ratio went from 17% to 30%.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Closing Stock in Trading Account = CMA FG Closing Balance. Also appears on B/S as Stock-in-trade 203617200.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Advertisement Expenses maps to CMA Advertisements and Sales Promotions.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 6 for CMA Admin Others — Telephone charges.
database_entries.validation_issue: Components sum to 27.65 (exact: 2765428.81 Rs = 27.654 lakhs, rounded to 27.65).

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 6 for CMA Admin Others — Generator/DG set running expenses.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 3 of 6 for CMA Admin Others — Miscellaneous expenses (catch-all).

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 4 of 6 for CMA Admin Others — Delivery/logistics charges.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 5 of 6 for CMA Admin Others — Printing, stationery, bills, receipts.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 6 of 6 for CMA Admin Others — Professional/legal fees. Verification: 2.91 + 3.10 + 10.39 + 6.64 + 4.00 + 0.62 = 27.66 in lakhs. Exact Rs: 290565.44 + 310183.17 + 1039142.39 + 663928.81 + 400109 + 61500 = 2765428.81 = 27.65 lakhs ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 2 for CMA Admin R&M — general repairs and maintenance. 903913.08 + 45517.00 = 949430.08 = 9.49 lakhs ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 2 for CMA Admin R&M — vehicle maintenance (delivery van). 903913 + 45517 = 949430 = 9.49 lakhs ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a PARTNERSHIP firm, 'Salary to Partners' maps to CMA 'Audit Fees & Directors Remuneration' because there are no directors. The CMA row name is a misnomer for partnerships — it represents remuneration to the managing partners (equivalent of directors). In a Pvt Ltd company, this row would contain actual director remuneration and audit fees. Note: audit fees are not separately disclosed in this P&L.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Partners' salary of 10.80 lakhs (6 partners: max allowed u/s 40(b) = 10.80L per ITR computation). Maps to CMA 'Audit Fees & Directors Remuneration'.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — 'By Other Interest' 82454 = CMA Interest Received 0.82 lakhs. Also appears in Computation of Income under 'Income from Other Sources'.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Profit from acquired business Kurinji Metro shown separately in P&L credit side. Acquisition happened during FY25. This is the net profit attributable to Metro operations post-acquisition.

database_entries.cma_field_name: Interest on Fixed Loans / Term loans
database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.industry_note: In a PARTNERSHIP firm, Interest to Partners (@ 6% p.a. per ITR) is mapped to CMA 'Interest on Fixed/Term Loans' because partners' capital and loans are treated as term liabilities. The CMA Queries sheet explicitly states: 'Interest to Partners should be under Term Loan Interest'. In a Pvt Ltd company, this row would contain interest on actual bank term loans.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Interest to Partners 23.18 = CMA Term Loan Interest 23.18. Partners' interest at 6% on total capital/loan balance of ~386 lakhs (approximate). Confirmed by CMA Queries sheet Row 14.

database_entries.sheet_name: Trading and Profit and Loss Account
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — entire Bank Interest and Charges to WC Interest. Primary component is ICICI Cash Credit (account 608151000628) interest on WC limit of 765.53 lakhs.
database_entries.validation_issue: The P&L shows 'Bank Interest and Charges' as a combined item. In CMA, the CA mapped the entire amount to 'Interest on WC loans' with 0 for Bank Charges (INPUT_R84). Ideally, bank charges (processing fees, DD charges, etc.) should be separated. The CMA Queries sheet asks for a breakup of this amount.

database_entries.raw_text: Income Tax provision (including interest on delayed payment)
database_entries.sheet_name: Computation of Tax
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA tax provision (23.21) includes both tax liability (21.27 = 30% of 68.18 + 4% cess) AND interest on delayed payment (1.94 = 234B + 234C interest). Normally, interest on tax delay should not be part of tax provision. The CA included it as a single figure.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: In a PARTNERSHIP firm, 'Partners' Capital Account' maps to 'Share Capital' in CMA. There are no shares — this represents total capital contributed by 6 partners. Profit sharing: A.Birthouskhan 10%, B.Bharathnisha 20%, B.Shafiqkhan 10%, S.Arfana Begam 25%, S.Fathima Foumiya 25%, B.Shakirkhan 10%. Opening capital was 386.41 (FY24) and increased by 534.54 (new contributions + retained profit) to 920.96.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Partners' Capital Account in B/S = CMA Share Capital. Partnership entity: capital accounts serve the same role as share capital in companies.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — ICICI Cash Credit account balance = CMA Working Capital Bank Finance. This is the primary WC facility from ICICI Bank.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 2 for CMA Term Loan — older ICICI term loan nearing repayment. 8.68 + 285.19 = 293.87 ≈ 293.86 (rounding ±0.01) ✓
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA Queries (Row 11) asks for repayment schedule of this loan. Outstanding as on 31 Mar 2025: 8.68 lakhs.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 2 for CMA Term Loan — new ICICI term loan for Kurinji Metro acquisition. This is the largest term loan component.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: In a partnership, partners' current accounts (separate from capital accounts) and acquisition loans from partners are treated as QUASI EQUITY in CMA — they represent long-term, unsecured partner funding that behaves like equity. The bank considers these as part of net worth for covenant calculations.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 2 for CMA Quasi Equity — Partners' current account (drawings/additional contributions float). 31.40 + 62.51 = 93.91 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: Partners' loan for Kurinji Metro acquisition — treated as quasi equity because it's a partner-funded business acquisition, not expected to be repaid in the near term.
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 2 for CMA Quasi Equity — partners' acquisition loan. 31.40 + 62.51 = 93.91 ✓

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Component 1 of 2 for CMA Unsecured LTD — Inter-group loan from Kurinji Maxx (related entity). 18.63 + 189.35 = 207.98 ✓
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA Queries sheet (Row 15) asks 'What happens to loan taken from Kurinji Maxx?' — suggesting repayment status is uncertain. Classified as long-term debt, not quasi equity.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Component 2 of 2 for CMA Unsecured LTD — Loan from Nisk Reetail Ventures. 18.63 + 189.35 = 207.98 ✓
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA Queries sheet (Row 16) asks 'When is loan taken from Nisk repayable?' — repayment timeline unknown. Nisk Reetail Ventures is a related entity.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: For a PARTNERSHIP firm following Old GAAP with WDV depreciation method, the B/S shows fixed assets at Written Down Value (not at cost less accumulated depreciation). So the CMA 'Gross Block' figure IS the WDV. The CMA shows Accumulated Depreciation as 0 for audited years because the block IS already net of depreciation. Opening WDV: 317.47 + Additions: 176.13 - Depreciation: 50.62 = Closing WDV: 442.99.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Fixed Assets in B/S at WDV = CMA Gross Block. Asset classes: Furniture/Fittings (10%), Building (5%), Plant & Machinery (15%), Computers (40%), plus Metro acquisition blocks.
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA Queries (Row 8) notes 'Schedule for fixed assets not matching' — likely because the book WDV and IT WDV may differ slightly.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: In a retail supermarket, 'Stores and Spares' inventory = packing materials (bags, wrapping, containers). This is specific to the trading industry — manufacturing companies would have actual spare parts here.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Stock of Packing Materials on B/S = CMA Stores and Spares inventory.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: In a trading company, all stock-in-trade = finished goods in CMA. There is no raw material or WIP inventory. The audit report notes stock valuation could not be verified due to the nature of supermarket inventory (large number of items, small quantities). Stock jumped from 697.72 to 2036.17 due to Kurinji Metro acquisition.
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Stock-in-trade on B/S = CMA Finished Goods inventory. Also matches P&L Closing Stock 203617200.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Cash on hand from B/S cash details = CMA Cash on Hand.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Deposits and Advances on B/S = CMA Other Advances/current asset.
database_entries.validation_issue: This is a large current asset (330.60 lakhs = 48% of sales) with no detailed breakdown in the B/S. Likely includes security deposits with landlords, advance tax, and other business deposits. The CMA treats it entirely as current asset.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.industry_note: Kurinji Shopping is a related entity (same promoter group). The advance to Kurinji Shopping is classified as 'Dues from directors/partners/promoters' in CMA because it's an inter-group advance to a promoter-linked entity. In CMA, this is a NON-CURRENT ASSET (not expected to be recovered within 1 year). CMA Queries (Row 17) asks 'When shall we get our loan advanced to Kurinji Shopping?'
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Kurinji Shopping advance on B/S = CMA Dues from partners under non-current assets. Group company advance classified as promoter-linked receivable.

database_entries.sheet_name: Balance Sheet
database_entries.reasoning: Direct match — Sundry Creditors in B/S = CMA Sundry Creditors for goods. Represents amounts owed to FMCG/grocery suppliers.
database_entries.validation_issue: CMA Queries ask for category-wise party-wise breakup of sundry creditors for both FY24 and FY25.
